citations.text: Darwin, C. (1859). On the Origin of Species.

en.biology: Differential reproductive success among heritable variants within a population, resulting from their interaction with the environment.

examples: Peppered moth coloration shifting with industrial soot levels.
Antibiotic resistance increasing in bacterial populations under drug exposure.
nonExamples: An individual organism changing behavior within its own lifetime (not heritable).
A population's gene frequencies changing due to a random sampling event (see Drift).
